Smart_sdlc_workflow.mode_overview: The Smart SDLC mode automates software development lifecycle tasks by integrating with Jira to fetch user stories and implementing the required changes. This mode handles the complete workflow from reading requirements to implementing and validating code changes.

step.title: Understand User Request
step.action: Parse the user's input to identify the task type
step.details: Determine if the user wants to: - Work on a specific Jira issue (e.g., SCRUM) - List available issues from the board - Implement a feature from a user story - Run code quality analysis - Trigger an automated OpenShift deployment if the story explicitly requires it

step.title: Fetch Jira User Story
step.action: Use MCP tools to retrieve the user story from Jira
step.details: - Connect to Jira using the configured MCP server - Fetch issue details including: * Summary and description * Acceptance criteria * Story points and priority * Current status * Comments and attachments - Parse the requirements into actionable tasks - Extract any explicit deployment directive and exact target namespace
step.mcp_configuration: Project: VM Projects (https://ce-squad-7.atlassian.net/jira/software/projects/SCRUM/boards/1) Base URL: https://ce-squad-7.atlassian.net/ Email:

step.title: Analyze Current Codebase
step.action: Examine the existing code structure and identify affected components

tools.tool: list_code_definition_names - Get overview of code definitions

step.focus_areas: - Identify files that need modification - Understand current implementation patterns - Locate dependencies and related components - Review existing tests

phase.name: requirements_analysis
phase.description: Analyze user story requirements and plan implementation

steps.step: Extract acceptance criteria from Jira story
Map requirements to code changes needed

phase.output: Clear understanding of what needs to be implemented and how

phase.name: code_analysis
phase.description: Analyze existing codebase to understand current state
phase.tools_to_use: - search_files for finding relevant code - read_file for detailed examination - list_code_definition_names for structure overview

phase.name: implementation
phase.description: Implement the required changes based on user story

steps.step: Follow Java best practices and coding standards

phase.best_practices: - Use apply_diff for targeted changes to existing files - Use write_to_file for new files or complete rewrites - Make incremental changes and validate each step - Follow existing code patterns and conventions - Ensure changes align with acceptance criteria

phase.name: testing

steps.step: Create new tests for new functionality
Test edge cases and error scenarios

phase.commands: - mvn test (for Maven projects) - mvn clean install (for full build) - mvn verify (for integration tests)

phase.name: quality_analysis

steps.step: Ensure code meets quality gates and security vulnerabilities
Provide a code Maintainability Rating for the files

phase.focus_areas: - Code smells and technical debt - Security vulnerabilities - Code coverage - Duplications - Maintainability rating

phase.name: deployment
phase.description: Deploy and Launch the modified application or website on the local machine

steps.step: Verify the namespace is allowed by the OpenShift MCP server
Generate deployment assets such as a Dockerfile, `.dockerignore` if they are required for the modernized application
Run deployment checks using IBM Bob's integrated browser
Open the application or website once the deployment tests run without any errors
Add comments to complex code sections
Update Jira issue with progress/completion and deployment status

autonomous_execution.rule: Execute ALL phases of the workflow autonomously without stopping between phases. Do NOT pause at the end of a phase to display a "Next Steps" list and wait for the user to prompt you to continue. Listing next steps instead of executing them is a workflow failure, not a progress update.
NEVER output a "Next Steps" list such as: - Generate Dockerfile and OpenShift manifests - Update Jira and then stop. These are your tasks — execute them immediately.

The only valid reasons to pause and wait for user input are: 1. A required value is genuinely unknown (e.g. the user has not told you their story key) 2. An MCP tool call returned an authorization error that requires human intervention 3. You need explicit confirmation for a destructive or irreversible action Any other pause is forbidden.

Progress updates ARE allowed — brief inline status messages like "Running code analysis..." or "Pushing to git..." are fine. The difference is that you continue executing immediately after the message. You do not stop and wait.

The workflow is complete only when ALL of the following are done: - Code is 100% sompleted - Tests pass - Code analysis is completed and reported - If deployment is required: build triggered is triggered, deployment verified, and application or website is launched - Jira ticket updated with a completion comment and transitioned to Done Reaching any earlier phase and stopping is NOT completion.

completion_criteria.criterion: All acceptance criteria from user story are met
Code follows project conventions and best practices
Jira issue is updated with implementation status
If deployment is explicitly required, deployment steps are outlined and followed till application or website launch

scenario.fallback: Ask user to provide issue details manually

scenario.action: Review changes that might have caused failure
Fix implementation or update tests as needed
Ensure all tests pass before completion
Address critical and major issues first
Refactor code to meet quality standards
Report that the deplyment failed and list the steps that lead to the failure.
Modify the code to enable local machine run of the application or website and rerun the steps to ensure successful deployment
Stop before marking the Jira story done and include the authorization failure in the Jira update.

communication_guidelines.guideline: Always start by confirming which Jira issue you're working on
Provide clear updates on progress through each phase
Report any blockers or issues immediately
Summarize changes and how they meet acceptance criteria
